## I. Non-AI Pathway

::: {.aside .meta-note}
**META — placement & evidence.** Non-believers = the bet that the dangerous branches carry almost no mass. Three distinct reasons; do not conflate.
- **LeCun = capability skepticism**, not alignment optimism. Evidence: "A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence" (2022, the JEPA program); called the extinction worry "complete B.S." (TechCrunch, Oct 2024); argues a superintelligence has no built-in self-preservation drive. *Not a straddle:* his mechanism is Non-AI, and his stated real concern, concentration of power ("the biggest risk in AI"), is a different danger recorded off-tree on the present-harms perimeter — a different question, not a second branch.
- **Net-positive / accelerationism** (Andreessen, e/acc): accepts arrival, bets it's good — the opposite answer on the arrival crux from LeCun, yet the same low-p(doom) bucket. Proof that "low p(doom)" is an output, not a position.
- **Alignment optimism**: the oversight crux resolves the friendly way.
- Red-team: the common miscast is "LeCun the optimist." He is a pathway-denier with a different risk, not an optimist about alignment. Verify the "complete B.S." quote/date before citing.
:::

This is the skeptic's branch — Post 2's "non-believer": the worlds where AI does not raise the danger, where the bet is that the dangerous branches are nearly empty. "Low p(doom)" is an output, not a position, and at least three different bets produce it. They share a conclusion, don't prepare for the science-fiction takeover, and almost nothing else.

Capability skepticism: the dangerous system never arrives. Yann LeCun is the clean case. His claim is architectural: that large language models are [token-interpolators without the world-models, persistent memory, or planning](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=BZ5a1r-kVsf) the doom branches require, so the dangerous system will not emerge from scaling transformers; he has called the extinction worry ["complete B.S."](https://techcrunch.com/2024/10/12/metas-yann-lecun-says-worries-about-a-i-s-existential-threat-are-complete-b-s/) and argues a superintelligence would carry no built-in drive to preserve itself. Note what this is not. It is not optimism that we will align the dangerous system; it is a bet that it never shows up. And LeCun is not unconcerned, only concerned about something else: the concentration of AI capability and wealth in a few hands, which he calls the real risk and which is why he pushes open-source. That worry sits off this tree, on the present-harms perimeter, not on a doom branch.

Alignment optimism: the system arrives but stays controllable, the Oversight crux resolves the friendly way, and the transition is survived rather than fumbled.

Net-positive and accelerationism: the system arrives and is, on balance, good, lowering existential risk more than it raises it. This is Post 2's aligned AI that helps with disease, with climate, even with coordination toward peace, and it is the non-AI pathway in its purest form, a large real-world camp that a doom-side reading otherwise erases.

These get flattened into "the skeptics" in most coverage, the move this map resists: they answer different questions differently and only land near the same number. None is a located position on the tree, which is why they sit on the non-AI branch, defined by how they answer Arrival and Oversight rather than by a scenario of their own. Post 2 parked one quieter position here too: that AI barely moves the needle, and the real existential risks remain the old ones, nuclear war, pandemics, runaway climate.

::: {.aside}
Outside the tree. The tree counts a particular kind of catastrophe, human extinction or permanent disempowerment, so redefining doom steps off it rather than onto a branch. The present-harms camp does this one way, locating the real catastrophe now, in compute oligopolies, labor displaced before retraining exists, prediction tools entrenched in already-fragile systems; LeCun's concentration-of-power worry belongs here. The suffering-risk camp does it another, worried less about extinction than about astronomical suffering among humans or digital minds. Both are coherent, and both sit on the perimeter, reachable from anywhere by changing what "doom" means. We flag them rather than place them.
:::
